spec_match.rationale: The live wrapper uses a container-name list and descendant rules such as @container mode-dark, with no container-type set, matching the ChromeStatus headline behavior.

interactive.rationale: Theme buttons, density and brand selects, a preview-width range, and a no-name edge checkbox all update the live container and readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-page blurb now describes the container-name list and no-container-type behavior shown by the concept.

edge_cases.rationale: The page probes real name-only support and includes an explicit container-name:none state that falls back to the base card styling.

references.rationale: The references section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5184267522015232.

design_system.rationale: Local colors use shared CSS variables and color-mix, button overrides set background and color together, and the page remains a real interactive demo rather than a code card.

summary: Resolved the major missing-reference critique and rebuilt the concept around real name-only container queries: controls compose the live container-name list, @container rules match those names without container-type, support detection reports fallback behavior, and a no-name edge state shows graceful degradation.
